stories.title: **Headline:** A&M Stuns No. 1 OU in Game 1 — Series Finale (Games 2 & 3) Decides SEC Title Today

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: No. 12 A&M erased a 5-3 OU lead with a 5-run 6th to stun No. 1 Oklahoma 8-5 in Game 1. Games 2 AND 3 are TODAY in College Station. Kendall Wells at 36 HRs — one from the all-time NCAA record. #Sooners #GigEm

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Two games. One series. Massive SEC title implications. #Sooners need a sweep in College Station today to clinch. Can Kendall Wells tie Lauren Espinoza's 31-year NCAA HR record (37) in the process? Watch on ESPN2 at 4 PM CT. #GigEm

article.headline: A&M Stuns No. 1 Oklahoma 8-5 — Games 2 and 3 Decide Series and SEC Title Today

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
#### Image Concept 1 — Social (1080x1350)
**Type:** gemini / complete
**Story:** A&M Stuns OU — Series Finale Today
**Visual:** Split graphic — left side shows Oklahoma crimson batter in power stance, right side shows A&M maroon crowd in celebration. Bold headline text: "SERIES NOT OVER" in white on dark overlay. Sub-text: "Games 2 & 3 Today | College Station". Dramatic lighting, dynamic energy. ILS brand colors in footer.
**Mood:** High drama, urgency, must-watch
**Text overlay:** "SERIES NOT OVER — Games 2 & 3 Today"

#### Image Concept 2 — Article Hero (1200x630)
**Type:** gemini / complete
**Story:** A&M Stuns OU
**Visual:** Softball diamond aerial view with A&M and OU helmets on either dugout side, scoreboard showing 8-5 A&M, dramatic sunset lighting over Davis Diamond. Text overlay: "GAME 1: A&M 8, OU 5 — PLAY RESUMES TODAY"
**Mood:** Tension, rivalry, high stakes

stories.title: **Headline:** Nebraska's Big Ten Title Doubleheader — 13-Game Streak on the Line at Penn State

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Nebraska (40-6, 20-1 Big Ten) plays a DOUBLEHEADER at Penn State today — 10 AM and 1:30 PM CT on BTN. Win two and clinch the Big Ten regular season title. 13-game win streak on the line. #Huskers

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: The Huskers need this one. Nebraska at Penn State, doubleheader today on Big Ten Network. A Big Ten title + national seed to host a regional at Bowlin Stadium is in sight for 40-6 Nebraska. #Huskers #BigTen

article.headline: Nebraska Faces Penn State Doubleheader — Big Ten Title and National Seed on the Line

stories.title: **Headline:** SEC Tournament Seeding Battle — Double Byes at Stake as Regular Season Closes

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: SEC Tournament seedings are almost set. Top 4 get double byes in Lexington starting Tuesday. OU leads at 18-4. Florida 16-5. Alabama 15-5. Today's OU-A&M finale finishes it off. May 5-9 at John Cropp Stadium. #SECSoftball

article.headline: SEC Tournament Seedings Take Shape — Double Byes at Stake as Regular Season Ends

stories.title: **Headline:** Texas Tech 47-4 Heads to Oklahoma City — Big 12 Tournament Preview

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: Texas Tech (47-4) heads to Devon Park, OKC as the No. 1 seed in the Big 12 Tournament (May 7-9). NiJaree Canady has 1,000+ career Ks and is the most dominant pitcher in the country. #WreckEm #Big12Softball

article.headline: Texas Tech 47-4 Enters Big 12 Tournament as No. 1 Seed — NiJaree Canady Leads the Way

stories.title: **Headline:** No. 10 FSU Closes Regular Season at Boston College — ACC Title and National Seed Push

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: No. 10 Florida State (43-8) closes the regular season at Boston College this weekend. With the ACC Tournament set for May 6-9 in Charlottesville, the Seminoles are pushing for a national seed. #FSUSoftball #ACCSoftball

stories.title: **Headline:** Conference Tournament Roadmap — All Five Major Leagues Tip Off Next Week

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text: Conference tournament week is almost here. SEC: May 5-9 Lexington. Big Ten: May 6-9 College Park. Big 12: May 7-9 OKC. ACC: May 6-9 Charlottesville. Selection Show: May 10, 7 PM ET on ESPN2. WCWS: May 28. 🥎

stories.title: **Headline:** WCWS Countdown — 26 Days to Devon Park

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text: 26 days to the WCWS. Devon Park, OKC. May 28–June 3. The Selection Show on May 10 reveals 64 teams. New in 2026: 32-team WCWS seeding. Projected national seeds include OU, TTech, Nebraska, Florida, Alabama and FSU. 🥎 #WCWS
